name: Mountains to Sea (Nga Ara Tuhono)
description: A classic multi-day journey from Mt Ruapehu toward the coast with varied terrain and big scenery.
difficultyExplanation: This route combines varied trail quality and moderate climbing over several days. Conditions are usually manageable, but planning for weather and support points still matters.
beAwareOf: Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress.
tips: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer.
trickiestSection: The middle section has changing surfaces and weather exposure, so pace the day conservatively and keep a margin before the final remote pushes.
routeSummary: Mountains to Sea (Nga Ara Tuhono) is a intermediate bikepacking route covering about 215 km, commonly ridden over 5 days. A classic multi-day journey from Mt Ruapehu toward the coast with varied terrain and big scenery. Riders can expect 55% Gravel / 25% Road / 20% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Oct-Apr, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: The middle section has changing surfaces and weather exposure, so pace the day conservatively and keep a margin before the final remote pushes. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
recommendedSetupText: For this 5-day route, we recommend a Gravel Bike to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
